zh.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton是Sir Thomas de Merton和WIGER的兒子，於1279年出生在英國英格蘭戴文格亞特 托林頓。他於大約1309年與Margaret de Clevedon在英國英格蘭戴文默頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1342年在他的家鄉享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton是Sir Thomas de Merton和WIGER的儿子，于1279年出生在英国英格兰戴文格亞特 托林頓。他于大约1309年与Margaret de Clevedon在英国英格兰戴文默頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1342年在他的家乡享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Merton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or more of the many places called Merton, such as found in Surrey, Kent, Devon, Oxfordshire, and Norfolk. The placename derives from Old English mere ‘pool’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. There has probably been some confusion with both Marton and Murton .

variants.name: Marton
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Antal, Arpad, Geza, Andras, Bela, Denes, Endre, Gaza, Imre, Istvan, Janos.

English: habitational name from any of several places so called, principally in Lincolnshire, Warwickshire, and North Yorkshire, named in Old English as ‘settlement by a lake’ (from mere or mær ‘pool, lake’ + tūn ‘settlement’) or as ‘settlement by a boundary’ (from (ge)mære ‘boundary’ + tūn ‘settlement’). Compare Martin 2.
Hungarian (Márton): from the personal name Márton (see Martin 1). This surname is also found in Romania; see also 3 below.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton was born in 1279, in Great Torrington, Devon, England, United Kingdom as the son of Sir Thomas de Merton and WIGER. He married Margaret de Clevedon about 1309, in Merton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1342, in his hometown, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton est né en 1279, à Great Torrington, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Sir Thomas de Merton et WIGER. Il a épousé Margaret de Clevedon environ 1309, à Merton, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1342, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Mertonは1279年にイギリスイギリスデボングレアト トリントンでSir Thomas de MertonとWIGERの息子として生まれた。約1309年にイギリスイギリスデボンマートンでMargaret de Clevedonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1342年に故郷で63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton(은)는 1279년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 그리트 토링턴에서 Sir Thomas de Merton(와)과 WIGER의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1309년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 머턴에서 Margaret de Clevedon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1342년 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Richard I de Merton родился в 1279 г. в местности грит торрингтон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Sir Thomas de Merton и WIGER. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Clevedon около 1309 г. в местности миртон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1342 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет.
